mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 8.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining INDPRO are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 4% signals a strengthening demand environment that increases corporate profitability and investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 4% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in real GDP above 4% signal a monetary easing cycle that strengthens the demand environment and compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with both FEDFUNDS decline and GDPC1 increase are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% and a 2-month decline in industrial production signal a weakening labor market and supply chain that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both UNRATE > 8.5% and INDPRO decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 4% and a 2-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signal a strengthening demand environment and housing market that increases corporate profitability and investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both GDPC1 > 4% and HOUST > 1.2 million are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in the consumer price index above 2.5% signal a monetary easing cycle that strengthens the demand environment and increases inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with both FEDFUNDS decline and CPIAUCSL increase are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
